title: ProtocolLab Runner Baseline Verification
scope: This verification covers the current Runner v1 baseline checks and the expected evidence bundle for future runner extraction work. It does not claim raw QUIC, WebTransport, MASQUE, database workload, nginx, Caddy, quic-go, network simulation, or publishable isolated-host benchmark coverage.
verification_method: Run repository build and test commands, exercise ProtocolLab check, validate an existing HTTP scenario, run an existing local HTTP/3 or HTTP/1 scenario, generate a report, inspect artifacts, and validate canonical SpecTrace JSON.
preconditions: Optional Docker-backed stages are either provisioned or explicitly skipped.
Existing HTTP scenarios and implementation manifests are present in the repository.
No raw QUIC execution work is added for this verification pass.
procedure: Run `dotnet run --project src\Incursa.ProtocolLab.Cli -- validate --implementations kestrel-http3 --scenarios http.core.plaintext --protocol h1 --run-id runner-baseline-validate --output .artifacts\runs` or an equivalent existing HTTP validation scenario.

Run `dotnet run --project src\Incursa.ProtocolLab.Cli -- run --implementations kestrel-http3 --scenarios http.core.plaintext --protocol h1 --run-id runner-baseline-run --output .artifacts\runs` or an equivalent existing local HTTP/3 scenario when local H3 prerequisites are available.
Inspect `.artifacts\runs\runner-baseline-run` for `result.json`, `validation.json`, raw load-tool stdout and stderr files, `aggregate-results.json`, and `summary.md` when benchmarking was attempted.
expected_result: Restore, build, tests, check, validation, run, report, artifact inspection, and SpecTrace validation complete without fabricated benchmark data; unsupported or unavailable stages are recorded explicitly with reasons.
evidence: `dotnet build Incursa.ProtocolLab.sln --no-restore` completed successfully with 0 warnings and 0 errors after runner host/API cleanup.
`dotnet test Incursa.ProtocolLab.sln --no-build` passed 126/126 tests after adding structured result/event contract tests and architecture guardrail coverage.
`dotnet run --project src\Incursa.ProtocolLab.Cli -- list implementations --root .` completed successfully and listed existing implementation manifests.

`dotnet run --project src\Incursa.ProtocolLab.Cli -- check --root .` completed successfully; h2load process mode was unavailable, oha process mode was available, Docker was available, and curl HTTP/3 proof remained unsupported.

`dotnet run --project src\Incursa.ProtocolLab.Cli -- validate --root . --implementations kestrel-http3 --scenarios http.core.plaintext --protocol h1 --run-id runner-baseline-validate --output .artifacts\runs` completed successfully with validation passed.

`dotnet run --project src\Incursa.ProtocolLab.Cli -- run --root . --implementations kestrel-http3 --scenarios http.core.plaintext --protocol h1 --duration 1 --warmup 0 --repetitions 1 --run-id runner-baseline-run --output .artifacts\runs` completed successfully with validation passed, benchmark succeeded, and loadTool=oha.
`dotnet run --project src\Incursa.ProtocolLab.Cli -- report --root . --run-id runner-baseline-run --output .artifacts\runs` completed successfully and printed the generated summary.
Architecture guardrail tests confirmed the runner project does not reference System.CommandLine, does not directly write to Console, and keeps CLI console rendering in the CLI project.

Runner contract tests confirmed check returns structured messages, validation failure returns a structured failed result, run returns artifact references, and RecordingRunnerEventSink captures structured status events without a CLI console.
status_summary: Runner baseline verification passed for the hardened runner boundary. The successful local run used the existing Kestrel HTTP/1 plaintext scenario and oha process load tool; it is local comparability evidence only, not publishable benchmark evidence.
